data.name: EnemyNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12163
data.value: - Once a Kukulkan decides to use Bombination on a player, it will slowly move close enough for the AoE to hit the player before starting the cast. It will keep following until it gets within range, even into other rooms! Be aware of these when fighting other mobs

data.value: - Can only be slowed with Arm's Length if transfigured via Pomander of Witching since it doesn't normally do melee auto-attacks

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7344
data.value: - Will not come into melee range if you stay away from it, doing very little damage

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7343
data.value: - Its line AoEs are quick, so be ready to move fast

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7350
data.value: - Offal Breath inflicts these debuffs: - Poison (magic DoT potency 10, 20s) - Nausea (20s) - Slow (20s) - Blind (30s) - Heavy (20s) - Silence (10s) - Paralysis (30s)

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7340
data.value: - auto-attacks inflict stacking poison (physical DoT potency 10 per stack, max 8 stacks, 30s)

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_71_7359
data.value: - Rotation: - Freefall (immediately when pulled) - Golden Talons x3 - Freefall - Winds of Winter - Be ready to stun or LoS

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_71_7357
data.value: - Be careful near mammoths when you've used a Pomander of Concealment, as Prehistoric Trumpet will break the effect

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_71_7358
data.value: - Does not make an effort to come into melee range unless Golden Tongue is interrupted

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_81_7373
data.value: - Rotation: - Goobbue's Grief - Moldy Phlegm - Goobbue's Grief - Inhale + Moldy Sneeze - Goobbue's Grief - Time for roughly 3 auto-attacks before it restarts (another Goobbue's Grief)

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_81_7375
data.value: - You can duck behind a corner as it uses Rush to steer it into the wall. You'll still take damage, but it can give you a chance to heal before it's in range to auto-attack - If LoSing Earthquake, you can peek out between stomps for uptime, but the timing is weird. Damage is applied when it hits the ground as expected, but it snapshots your position earlier, as its feet come off the ground

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_81_7374
data.value: - If you let it do one auto-attack after Vile Utterance and then duck behind a corner, you should avoid the vulnerability up

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_81_7367
data.value: - You can duck behind a corner as it uses Buffet to steer it into the wall. You'll still take damage, but it can give you a chance to heal before it's in range to auto-attack - For melee jobs, try standing behind it during its casts to delay its next auto-attack

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_81_7365
data.value: - These mostly just spam abilities, doing very little auto-attack damage - Try to stand behind it during Dragon's Voice and Breath attacks to delay its next auto-attack - Voice and Breath abilities are all treated as spells, so an impeding trap can interrupt one in an emergency

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_81_7366
data.value: - Hits hard, and doesn't stop to use abilities so the damage is constant - DO NOT pull while it has the attack bonus - Be very careful fighting other enemies nearby

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_81_7370
data.value: - Elbow Drop can be baited to delay its auto-attacks; just don't get hit by it!

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_91_7391
data.value: - Rare spawn

data.name: EnemyNote_HeavenOnHigh_91_7389
data.value: - Each time it grows, it gains a stacking damage up buff - Pattern: - Drainstrikes - Iron Justice - Cloudcover x2 - Grows - Iron Justice x2 - Grows

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_001
data.value: - The arena is a 3x3 grid of mines (pachypodium mine adds) - Rotation (53s per cycle): - Authoritative Shriek - Authoritative Shriek - Mandrastorm - Mines activate in rows with the middle always being first. Stand in the third row to activate and move into the first after it explodes. - At level 85, a Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 7m15s with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_011
data.value: - Initial actions: - Offensive Command: summons 3 adds in a Y shape - Stand beside any of the adds - Offensive Command: summons 5 more adds to complete the circle - Stand beside the one tethered add that doesn't have another tether across from it - Piercing Laser - Order Relay - see note below for safe spots - Rotation (59s per cycle): - Piercing Laser - Piercing Laser - Offensive Command for 5 random adds - Stand beside a tethered add that doesn't have another tether across from it - Order Relay - see note below for safe spots - The two sets of 4 adds for Order Relay will each be a group of 3 and a solo add, separated by the solo add for the other set. To dodge the flames, start in front of the second solo add, then move to the first solo add after the first flames are done - A Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 6m with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_021
data.value: - All damage other than the boss's auto-attack inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m) - Initial actions: - Creature of Darkness - Dark Wyrmtail OR Dark Wyrmwing - Dark Megaflare - Whei Morn - Dark Wyrmwing OR Dark Wyrmtail - Rotation (2m12s per cycle): - Creature of Darkness - Dark Wyrmtail OR Dark Wyrmwing - Whei Morn - Dark Wyrmwing OR Dark Wyrmtail - Dark Megaflare - Whei Morn - Dark Wyrmtail OR Dark Wyrmwing - The first two Dark Wyrm actions will be either tail followed by wing or wing followed by tail. Likewise, the 3 tail/wing actions in a single rotation will always be either tail-wing-tail or wing-tail-wing - A Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 7m45s with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_031
data.value: - Opening sequence: - Twister - Meracydian Cyclone - Meracydian Squall - Turbine - Twister - Meracydian Squall - Twisting Dive - Rotation after opening (42s per cycle): - Twister - Meracydian Cyclone - Meracydian Squall - Turbine - Getting hit by a Cyclone or Squall AoE inflicts vulnerability up (10% per stack, 30s) - The arena has an outer damage ring which inflicts windburn (unique DoT potency 200, 15s, cannot be removed with Esuna) if you step on it - Simple strategy for Meracydian Cyclone/Squall: - Find the open center-to-edge path for Turbine - Drop two Squall AoEs at the edge (the first one won't land until the third AoE appears) - Move inward, drop the other two Squall AoEs - Move to the center and wait for Turbine - A Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 4m30s with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_041
data.value: - Initial actions: - Songs of Ice and Thunder OR Thunder and Ice - Leftbreathed Thunder OR Rightbreathed Cold - Thunderous Cold OR Cold Thunder - Leftbreathed Thunder OR Rightbreathed Cold - Rotation (1m18s per cycle): - Cacophony - Leftbreathed Thunder OR Rightbreathed Cold - Thunderous Cold OR Cold Thunder - Leftbreathed Thunder OR Rightbreathed Cold - Songs of Ice and Thunder OR Thunder and Ice - Elements not important for Left/Rightbreathed. For other abilities, pay attention to the order of elements in the ability name - ice/cold is pointblank and thunder is donut - All ice/thunder AoEs deal 900 potency magic damage (~50k to a DPS/healer) and inflict vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m) along with deep freeze (magic DoT potency 150, 6s) or paralysis (15s), uncurable with Esuna - Tanks can survive Ram's + Dragon's Voice combo with steel. DPS and healers may die to deep freeze DoT or a following auto-attack - Charge tether is potency 2700 when too close, which will one-shot DPS/healers through steel - If you are solo, your Cacaphony orb will always spawn west of center - A Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 4m45s with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_051
data.value: - Initial actions: - Octuple Swipe - Disorienting Groan OR Bullish Swing - Bullish Swipe (random target) - Rotation after opening (58s per cycle): - Thundercall - Octuple Swipe - Disorienting Groan OR Bullish Swing - Bullish Swipe (random target) - The boss rotates to face the player before Octuple Swipe, so the first swipe will always be toward the player. The first four swipes are in the order front > back > right > left (relative to boss orientation), and the next four are either the same order or reversed - The initial order is random, but it swaps for each following rotation. If you just saw forward order, the next rotation will be reverse order, and vice versa - Trick for Octuple Swipe: - Stand outside of the first spot - The only attack that might repeat in the same area is 4 and 5 - If there is no repeat, follow all of the attacks - always stand where the last one hit - If there was a repeat, follow the attacks until the one that repeats. Wait for both hits in that area, then move into it and stay there - Arm's Length or Surecast will block the Disorienting Groan knockback, but since each rotation takes slightly less than a minute, the ability may not come back in time for the second next cycle - A Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 8m15s with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_061
data.value: - Rotation (1m13s per cycle): - Steel Claw - Ferocity (random target) - Preternatural Turn - Roar - Preternatural Turn - Preternatural Turn with crystals: - If the boss is doing a pointblank AoE, the crystals will do wide conal AoEs toward the outside of the arena. The safe spots are between the farthest crystal and an adjacent one, a bit closer to the far one and outside of the pointblank - If the boss is doing a donut AoE, the crystals will do pointblank AoEs. The safe spot is a small triangle just inside the inner circle of the boss's hitbox toward the far crystal - Unei can provide all the heals you need as long as you don't get hit by anything avoidable - A Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 10m15s with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_071
data.value: - Rotation (61s per cycle): - Resonance - Barofield (only used on first rotation) - Nanospore Jet - Nerve Gas (Centralized, Leftward, or Rightward) - Nerve Gas (Centralized, Leftward, or Rightward) - Auto-cannons fire during this cast - Nerve Gas (Centralized, Leftward, or Rightward) - Auto-cannons fire during this cast - Magnet tether appears - Nerve Gas (Centralized, Leftward, or Rightward) - Auto-cannons fire during this cast - Nerve Gas Ring - Magnet attract/repel happens just before this cast finishes; there is a quick moment to adjust when the telegraph appears - Auto-cannons fire at the same time - Getting hit by Auto-cannons or any Nerve Gas attack inflicts vulnerability up (10% per stack, 30s) - North and south adds are always negative. West and east adds are always positive - If you are solo, you will always be bound to the north add, which spawns behind the boss - Use steel if you're not confident, so you can easily survive a hit from any of the Nerve Gas abilities - A Doga demiclone will defeat the boss in about 10m45s with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_081
data.value: - Rotation (1m25s per cycle): - Support Systems; spawns: - 1 Interceptor α (egg) outside one side of the arena - 5 Interceptor β (cubes) in a row along one side of the arena, close to the egg - 1 Interceptor γ (sphere) inside the arena - Interception Sequence - dodge adds in the order of markers - Peripheral Lasers OR Cross Lasers - Laserstream - Support Systems; spawns: - 8 Interceptor β (cubes) along the north side of the arena - 8 Interceptor β (cubes) along the east side of the arena - Parallel Execution - dodge the circle AoEs while also getting to the safe side of both sets of firing cubes (wait for one to go off and run quickly into that space before the next goes off and hits you) - Support Systems; spawns: - 6 Interceptor β (cubes) along the west side of the arena with spaces beside the first and last at each end - 6 Interceptor β (cubes) along the north side of the arena with spaces beside the first and last at each end - 1 Interceptor α (egg) on the east side of the arena - 1 Interceptor α (egg) on the south side of the arena - Salvo Script - pay attention to the direction the eggs are facing - Safe spot is always in the far corner of a single square that is the second from the corner of the arena - If both eggs are facing inward, safe spot is between the two eggs - If one is facing toward the corner between the eggs, safe spot is on the far side of the other egg - Peripheral/Cross Lasers and Parallel Execution circle AoEs will one-shot DPS/healers through steel - Line AoEs during Parallel Execution (Aetherochemical Laser β) are survivable - All abilities except Laserstream inflict stacking vulnerability up (10% per stack, 1m) - An onion knight demiclone will defeat the boss in about 6m with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_EurekaOrthos_091
data.value: - Opening sequence: - Paradoxum - Caliburni - one set of blades - Thermal Divide - Blades from Caliburni fire back across the arena - Call Dimensional Blade - Solid OR Empty Souls' Caliber - Rotation after opening (1m20s per cycle): - Caliburni - 3 sets of blades - Paradoxum - one set of blades turns to fire, and another to ice - Frostforge OR Flameforge - you'll need to make sure your buff is opposite of this - Blades from Caliburni fire back across the arena. If you have the same element as the boss prepared, make sure to get hit by an opposite element blade - Solid OR Empty Souls' Caliber - Frost's OR Flame's Revelation, depending on which was prepared - Exglacialis OR Exflammeus - Thermal Divide - Soul of Fire/Ice increases damage from the same element to 10x normal, so everything becomes a one-shot - An onion knight demiclone will defeat the boss in about 10m with no player assistance

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_001
data.value: - Rotation: - Concussive Oscillation - Overtow from center of room - Amorphous Applause

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_011
data.value: - Rotation: - Proboscis - Psycho Squama - Proboscis - Neuro Squama - Butterflies appear along the perimeter at the same time - Psycho Squama - Butterflies all explode at the same time, inflicting big damage and paralysis - TLDR: Stay close to centre so everything is easy to dodge

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_021
data.value: - Opening sequence: - Lightning Strike - Cloud Call - Shiko - Make sure you're far from the boss and stand under a cloud. You will break the cloud, creating a safe spot, as the other clouds will all do AoEs a moment later - stay in the safe spot until after this - Lightning Strike (wait until cloud AoEs before dodging) - Rotation after opening sequence: - Lightning Strike - Lightning Strike - Supercell - Cloud Call - Shiko - Supercell (wait until cloud AoEs before dodging)

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_031
data.value: - Rotation: - Tornado - Ancient Aero II - Whirlwinds appear along the west and east sides of the arena - Ancient Aero III - Whirlwinds explode immediately after this, so make sure not get get knocked into them - Ancient Aero - Ancient Aero II - Whirlwinds appear along the north and south sides of the arena - Ancient Aero III - Ancient Aero

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_041
data.value: - Rotation: - Rusting Claw - he stops and raises a hand to indicate that he's readying this - Words of Woe - he stops, looks at the target, and stands still for a moment before firing this - Eye of the Fire - Orbs appear and boss drops proximity marker. Orbs will do telegraphed pointblank AoEs at regular intervals. Get away from the boss, but also away from the orbs - The Spin - big damage if you're close to the marker, and very little if you're half the room away - Orbs disappear as he does his next Rusting Claw

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_051
data.value: - Rotation: - Firewalker - First set of 4 Accursed Canes appears - Second set of 4 Accursed Canes appears - Fire II - at the same time, the first group of canes does donut AoEs - Second set of canes does donut AoEs - Topple - At the same time, the first group of canes does line AoEs at random players - Second group of canes does line AoEs - Canes disappear and a new group of 8 appears - Fire II - All 8 canes do donut AoEs. The centre is always safe from these, and there are safe spots to the outside of each cane - Ancient Flare - All 8 canes do line AoEs at random players. These are easiest to dodge if you bait them toward the walls - TLDR: Lots of AoEs to dodge

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_061
data.value: - Rotation: - Predator Claws - Slabber - Innerspace - Ululation - stay OUT of puddle until after this - Hound out of Hell - get IN the puddle when this targets you - Devour - stay IN puddle until this, then get OUT - Ululation - make sure you're OUT of puddle - TLDR: Be OUT of puddle for Ululation > IN for Hound out of Hell and Devour > OUT for Ululation - Innerspace targets a random player each round. Hound out of Hell and Devour are used on the same player - If you take no damage from Hound out of Hell, it will not stun you. Make sure not to leave the puddle until AFTER Devour

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_071
data.value: - Rotation: - Heavensward Howl - Eclipse Bite - Summons ice chunks that will explode in a large untelegraphed pointblank AoE if hit by the icicle, other exploding chunks, or Lunar Cry - Icicle appears on one side of the arena with telegraphed circle AoE, then does a telegraphed line AoE shooting across the platform - Lunar Cry - Eclipse Bite

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_081
data.value: - Rotation before 85%: - Burning Rave - Knuckle Press - Aura Cannon - Rotation after 85%: - Ancient Quaga - Meteor Impact - drops proximity marker under Onra; several black orbs also spawn around the room and pulse pointblank AoEs that inflict heavy - Aura Cannon - Burning Rave - meteor drops on proximity marker, and black orbs disappear roughly when this cast starts - Knuckle Press - Melee uptime strategy: - Place 3 markers along the wall, using the posts for reference: - A by the post at 3:00 - B 2 posts north/counter-clockwise from A - C 3 posts north/counter-clockwise from B (just right of the exit) - Do whatever for the first 15% - doesn't really matter. - Before Onra hits 85%, position him so that he's standing on the A marker and facing south - When he starts casting Ancient Quaga, go behind him. This prevents an auto-attack immediately after Ancient Quaga - Wait for Onra to turn around and make one auto-attack. This is important for positioning Meteor Impact - Immediately run just past B. You need to move right after the auto to avoid a black orb AoE. You want Onra to stop on the B marker - Wait for one auto-attack, then run to C, which will get you out of Aura Cannon - Wait for Burning Rave, then move to B - Wait for Knuckle Press, then move Onra back to A facing south - Repeat from Ancient Quaga - The delay from the Meteor Impact cast to the meteor actually dropping is random. Rarely it can drop *very* quickly, even before Aura Cannon, and deal potentially fatal damage. Steel can help mitigate this risk for jobs that can spare it

data.name: FloorsetNote_HeavenOnHigh_091
data.value: - You must touch the Empyrean Shrine at the far end of floor 100 to secure your victory. It takes about 15 seconds to run across the floor without Sprint - You gain the title for soloing once you touch the shrine. To set it for your screenshots while still inside, use this text command: /title set "Lone Hero"

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_001
data.value: - Rotation: - Whipcrack - Stormwind - Bombination - Lumisphere - Aero Blast

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_011
data.value: - Rotation: - Bloody Caress - Acid Mist - Gold Dust - Leafstorm - Boss runs to north end and stays there until after Rotten Stench phase - Two Palace Hornet adds appear - Rotten Stench x3 - Kill adds quickly, or they will use Final Sting, which does 70% max HP damage (each) - Lust can be used to AoE down the adds quickly while continuing to DPS the boss as well

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_021
data.value: - Auto-attacks are conal AoEs - Rotation: - Dissever - Ball of Fire - Ball of Ice - Dissever - Ball of Fire - Ball of Ice - Boss runs to center of room - Fear Itself - Make sure to stay out of middle for the fire/ice balls to keep a safe spot for avoiding Fear Itself

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_031
data.value: - Rotation: - Ancient Eruption (x4) - Adds: 2 Nightmare Bhoots appear - Entropic Flame - Accursed Pox - Entropic Flame - Accursed Pox - Scream - If adds are still alive at this point, prey will cause them to cast Tornado, likely killing you - Shadow Flare

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_041
data.value: - Rotation: - Until 90% health: - Dark Harvest - In Health (donut) - In Sickness - Uses Black Honeymoon once at 90% health - From 90% to 60% health: - Cold Feet - In Health (x2: pointblank, then donut) - Dark Harvest - In Sickness - Uses Black Honeymoon once at 60% health - Below 60% health: - Cold Feet - In Health (x2: pointblank > donut OR donut > pointblank) - Dark Harvest (x2) - In Health (donut) + adds - Summons untargetable adds outside the arena which will cast either a single circle AoE (potency 250) or four line AoEs (each potency 300), resolving shortly before In Health - Avoid the AoEs, then run toward Edda to dodge In Health - Black Honeymoon - Stay close to avoid In Health easily - But be careful of the disease debuff from In Sickness in the first half of the fight - Make sure to turn for Cold Feet

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_051
data.value: - Rotation: - Geirrothr - Hall of Sorrow - Valfodr - Flames appear around the edges of the arena and do telegraphed pointblank AoEs at the same time. Make sure to get knocked into a safe spot or use knockback immunity. The flames explode a couple seconds after the knockback, so there is a bit of time to adjust. Flame explosions deal 70% max HP damage - Geirrothr - Hall of Sorrow - Bleed DoT does not stack (standing in overlapping puddles does the same damage as standing in just one) - With sustaining potions (or other job utility) you can position yourself on the wall midway between two candles and just sit in the puddles for the entire fight - The flame AoE telegraphs are buggy and sometimes don't display - stay away from the flames even if you don't see the telegraphs

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_061
data.value: - Rotation: - Drench - Electrogenesis - Douse - Fang's End - Hit Sprint when it uses Drench if you're still heavied to escape Electrogenesis safely - Make sure to drag the boss out of the Douse puddles

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_071
data.value: - Rotation: - Thunderbolt - Charybdis - Thunderbolt - Trounce; runs to the east end of the arena to cast this - Charybdis - Thunderbolt - Charybdis - Trounce again, but from the west end - At 10% HP, the boss casts Ecliptic Meteor once, then returns to the rotation

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_081
data.value: - Rotation: - Scalding Scolding - Sap - Grey Bomb spawns along with several untargetable Lava Bombs - Grey Bomb starts using Burst immediately with a long cast time. It must be killed before this goes off to prevent big roomwide damage (80% max HP) - Lava Bombs all use Self-destruct after a few seconds. This is a telegraphed pointblank AoE, so don't get caught in the middle of them - Sap - Sap - Another Grey Bomb, but no Lava Bombs this time - Scalding Scolding - Sap - Sap - Giddy Bomb spawns along with several Lava Bombs as the boss starts casting Massive Burst - Massive Burst will do 99% max HP damage to everyone if it finishes - Giddy Bomb does a pointblank AoE that can interrupt the boss's Massive Burst. It can be moved around by attacking it - Lava Bombs are the same as before, and can get in the way of your attempts to move the Giddy Bomb if you're not prepared - Grey and Giddy Bomb spawn locations are static. Locations below are relative to the center and listed per rotation (Grey > Grey > Giddy) - Slightly south > center > slightly north-west - North > slightly south-west > slightly north-east - South > north > slightly north-east - Center > south > center - You can preposition the boss where Giddy Bomb will spawn to avoid having to move the bomb around - You can preposition the boss on first Grey Bomb and use Lust to AoE it along with the boss for a faster kill

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_91
data.value: - Timeline: - phase 1: uses only Abyss until HP reaches 90% - Summon Darkness: spawns 1 Giant Corse and 1 Bicephalic Corse - phase 2: uses Abyss and Shackle until HP reaches 65% - Summon Darkness: spawns 1 Bicephalic Corse and 1 Iron Corse - phase 3: uses Abyss, Shackle, and Word of Pain until HP reaches 40% - Summon Darkness: spawns 1 Iron, 1 Giant, and 1 Bicephalic Corse - phase 4: rotation of Doom > Abyss > Word of Pain > Shackle > Abyss > Word of Pain - Lust can be used to AoE the adds down quickly (3 hits) while also applying vulnerability stacks to the boss - Adds remain on the battlefield when killed, and will be resurrected after a while if not cleared with resolution - Resolution can be used to kill adds in one shot. It can also be used on the corpses to remove them from the battle, preventing them from resurrecting - You'll want to use at least one resolution to clear most, if not all, of the adds. They are likely to overwhelm you otherwise

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_101
data.value: - Rotation: - Whipcrack - Stormwind - Bombination - Lumisphere - Aero Blast

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_111
data.value: - Rotation: - Bloody Caress - Acid Mist - Gold Dust - Leafstorm - Boss runs to north end and stays there until after Rotten Stench phase - Two Deep Palace Hornet adds appear - Rotten Stench x3 - Full rotation takes 56 seconds - Kill adds quickly, or they will use Final Sting, which does 70% max HP damage (each). This can be outranged, but the adds will keep using it repeatedly until it hits or they die - If you use lust during the second Rotten Stench cast, it will last long enough to kill 2 sets of adds. Steel may be needed to survive long enough on non-tanks though - Rotten Stench will one-shot a DPS or healer without steel

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_121
data.value: - Auto-attacks are conal AoEs - Rotation: - Dissever - Ball of Fire - Ball of Ice - Dissever - Ball of Fire - Ball of Ice - Boss runs to center of room - Fear Itself - Make sure to stay out of middle for the fire/ice balls to keep a safe spot for avoiding Fear Itself - Fear Itself is a quicker cast than on floor 30, so get to the center fast

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_131
data.value: - Vulnerable to resolution - Can be killed in under 1 minute on any job using strength and resolution - Rotation: - Ancient Eruption (x4) - Adds: 2 Deep Palace Followers appear - Entropic Flame - Accursed Pox - Entropic Flame - Accursed Pox - Scream - If adds are still alive at this point, prey will cause them to cast Tornado, killing you with 99999 damage - Shadow Flare: roomwide AoE damage - Bleed pool DoT, Entropic Flame, and (presumably) Accursed Pox will one-shot DPS/healers - stay away

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_141
data.value: - Rotation: - Void Fire IV - Summon Darkness (Gargoyle): calls an untargetable gargoyle that will use a large telegraphed line AoE at the same time as Dark Mist - Dark Mist - Summon Darkness (Zombies): calls 4 zombie adds that will slowly crawl towards you. If one touches you, you will be bound until it dies - Void Fire IV - Void Aero - Summon Darkness (Vodoriga): calls an untargetable vodoriga that will use a telegraphed circle AoE just after the next Void Fire IV - Void Fire IV - Dark Mist - Summon Darkness (Succubus): calls a succubus add - in addition to autoattacks, uses Sweet Steel and Void Fire II - Void Aero - Void Fire IV - Fatal Allure - Blood Sword - the HP drain is 1:1, so whether you DPS the add or the boss doesn't matter in terms of damage - Blood Rain - kills any remaining zombies - Entire rotation is about 1m50s - Don't let the zombies catch you - Physical ranged jobs can just kite the zombies until they despawn. It's likely better for others to kill them. They have pretty low HP - If you're not killing the succubus add, it's worth putting DoTs on it anyway - In party play, Summon Darkness (Gargoyle/Vodoriga) summons one add for each party member

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_151
data.value: - Rotation: - Geirrothr - Hall of Sorrow - Valfodr - Flames appear around the edges of the arena and do telegraphed pointblank AoEs at the same time. Make sure to get knocked into a safe spot or use knockback immunity. The flames explode a couple seconds after the knockback, so there is a bit of time to adjust. Flame explosions deal 70% max HP damage - Geirrothr - Hall of Sorrow - Bleed DoT does not stack (standing in overlapping puddles does the same damage as standing in just one) - The flame AoE telegraphs are buggy and sometimes don't display - stay away from the flames even if you don't see the telegraphs

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_161
data.value: - Rotation: - Drench - Electrogenesis - Douse - Fang's End - Hit Sprint when it uses Drench if you're still heavied to escape Electrogenesis safely. Only needed 1 out of every 5 times because of diminshing returns on the heavy (15s > 7s > 3s > resist > resist > 15s) - If you're kiting, it is a good idea to try to pull the boss to the same place every time for Douse. If they're all stacked in one place, it's much easier to keep the boss out of them, reducing damage taken - If not kiting, simply pull him out of the puddle after Douse

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_171
data.value: - Rotation: - Thunderbolt - Charybdis (x2) - Thunderbolt - Trounce; runs to the south end of the arena to cast this - Charybdis (x2) - Thunderbolt - Charybdis - Trounce again, but from the north end - Enters enrage mode at 15% - stands up and casts Ecliptic Meteor, dealing 80% max HP damage to everyone in the party. Continues to cast this over and over until the fight ends - If you are standing in front of him during meteors, he can also autoattack you (so don't do that!) - Most jobs require a very strict strategy to get through this final push. See the job-specific notes below - Useful macros: - Cancel Lust: /statusoff "Transfiguration" - Useful if you have trouble clicking it off quickly - Cancel food: /statusoff "Well Fed" - Again useful if you have trouble cancelling it quickly otherwise - Cancelling food is useful during enrage if you're healing through it because Meteors do damage based on your max HP. Lower max HP means less damage taken, which means less damage to heal - Cancelling your food when not at full HP also acts like a pseudo heal since it lowers your max HP, which actually raises your current HP%

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_181
data.value: - Rotation: - Scalding Scolding - Remedy Bomb (blue) spawns along with several untargetable Giddy Bombs - Remedy Bomb starts using Hypothermal Combustion immediately with a long cast time. It must be killed before this goes off to prevent massive roomwide damage (80% max HP damage + 5s deep freeze) - Giddy Bombs all use Hypothermal Combustion after a few seconds. Theirs is a small telegraphed pointblank AoE with a short cast time, so stay away from them - Sap (x3) - Scalding Scolding - Sap - Lava Bomb (red) spawns along with several Giddy Bombs as the boss starts casting Massive Burst. - Massive Burst will do 99% max HP damage to everyone if it finishes - Lava Bomb does a pointblank AoE that can interrupt the boss's Massive Burst. It can be moved around by attacking it - Giddy Bombs are the same as before, and can get in the way of your attempts to move the Lava Bomb if you're not prepared - Full rotation takes 1 minute, 20 seconds - Remedy Bomb and Lava Bomb spawn locations are static. Knowing the spawn locations of Lava Bombs is especially useful, as you can then pull the boss to where it will spawn, allowing you to to keep uptime on the boss instead of running around to position the bomb - DO NOT open with lust if soloing, as the cleave at the start will destroy you - The safest time to use lust is during the triple Sap cast. Kill the Remedy Bomb as quickly as possible to maximize your time. DO NOT get greedy here. Drop transfiguration immediately regardless of stacks after the third Sap, as you need to be prepared for the cleave that follows - Another option is to use Lust to kill the Remedy Bomb and get stacks on the boss at the same time. It will take 4 hits to kill the Remedy Bomb, so don't delay

data.name: FloorsetNote_PalaceOfTheDead_191
data.value: - You must touch the Firecrest or Glass Pumpkin at the far end of floor 200 to secure your victory. It takes about 35 seconds to run across the floor without Sprint - You gain the title for soloing once you touch the ending. To set it for your screenshots while still inside, use this text command: /title set "The Necromancer"

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_31_Boss_31462

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_31_Boss_31465

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_Boss_1104

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_1_12240_31477
data.value: activates random mines, causing them to use Mandrashock

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_1_12240_31479
data.value: proximity AoE on boss - get away; minimum potency at 10y (1 grid square)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_1_12240_31478
data.value: telegraphed pointblank AoE; inflicts vulnerability up (25% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_11_12261_31799
data.value: orders some adds to use Flame Breath

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_11_12261_32545
data.value: orders half of the adds to use Flame Breath, followed shortly by the other half

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_11_12261_1227
data.value: telegraphed line AoE; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_11_12261_676
data.value: conal AoE with late telegraph; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_1105
data.value: untelegraphed donut AoE - get IN. Only used as part of the Songs and Thunderous Cold / Cold Thunder combos (never used on its own)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_1104
data.value: untelegraphed pointblank AoE - get OUT. Only used as part of the Songs and Thunderous Cold / Cold Thunder combos (never used on its own)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_31851
data.value: Ram's Voice followed by Dragon's Voice - get out, then in

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_31852
data.value: Dragon's Voice followed by Ram's Voice - get in, then out

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_31861
data.value: untelegraphed 180 degree AoE to the boss's left

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_31863
data.value: untelegraphed 180 degree AoE to the boss's right

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_31856
data.value: telegraphed charge AoE with tether, followed by Dragon's Voice, followed by Ram's Voice - get far to reduce charge damage, then get in, then out

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_31855
data.value: telegraphed charge AoE with tether, followed by Ram's Voice, followed by Dragon's Voice - get far to reduce charge damage, then get out, then in

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_1107
data.value: spawns an orb that tethers and chases each party member for a short time before stopping and exploding; runs to the middle to cast this

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_41_12265_1108
data.value: pointblank thunder AoE; used when the orb gets within range of its target or 9 seconds after it starts moving

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_51_12267_31872
data.value: Eight Bullish Swipe telegraphs followed by the actual attacks

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_51_12267_3972
data.value: knockback from center

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_51_12267_30744
data.value: 90 degree conal AoE with late telegraph - get behind; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_51_12267_31875
data.value: pointblank AoE with late telegraph - get away; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_51_12267_14463
data.value: roomwide AoE; leaves several orbs scattered around the room; orbs explode in pointblank AoEs in time with the first hit of Octuple Swipe

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_61_12246_10207
data.value: tankbuster

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_61_12246_31442
data.value: untelegraphed charge attack with tether; get far to reduce damage

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_61_12246_31436
data.value: donut AoE if boss has a big blue glowing animation; pointblank AoE otherwise; late telegraph. Inflicts vulnerability up (10% per stack, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_61_12246_6293
data.value: causes many rocks and crystals to fall from the ceiling causing circle AoEs; 4 crystals are left behind. Crystals do AoEs with late telegraphs with the next Preternatural Turn - see notes below

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_61_12246_516
data.value: pointblank AoE (magic damage) if boss has a big blue glowing animation; wide conal AoE (physical damage) otherwise; late telegraph. Inflicts vulnerability up (10% per stack, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_2923
data.value: conal AoE tankbuster

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_2927
data.value: creates damage zones in center (instant death) and outer ring (unique DoT potency 200) of arena

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_2939
data.value: inflicts positive or negative charge to each player and spawns four adds outside the arena with positive or negative charge markers; also re-creates the instant death zone in the center of the arena

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_2617
data.value: telegraphed line AoEs across the arena, forming an 'X'; used periodically - see rotation below

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_31423
data.value: wide conal AoE to the front; the boss turns to face the target before using this

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_31424
data.value: 180 degree AoE to the boss's left and behind

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_31425
data.value: 180 degree AoE to the boss's right and behind

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_71_12247_31426
data.value: donut AoE with late telegraph - only a narrow ring around the center is safe; instant death zone in center disappears right after this

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_31449
data.value: spawns Interceptor adds. α (eggs) will do wide conal AoEs, β (cubes) will do line AoEs, γ (spheres) will do a donut AoE, all with late telegraphs. The timing of these is determined by the boss's next move. See rotation for more details

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_31450
data.value: places 1/2/3 markers on the adds to indicate the order they will fire in

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_31454
data.value: causes adds in each set to fire one at a time from left to right while 5 consecutive circle AoEs also appear on players

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_31455
data.value: orders all adds to fire at once

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_31447
data.value: telegraphed donut AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_31448
data.value: cross AoE (lines through front-back and sides) - get to corners (oriented with boss/animation)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_31456
data.value: roomwide AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_81_12102_3943
data.value: AoE with late telegraph; shape varies with add (see rotation)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31353
data.value: roomwide AoE; grants Soul of Ice or Soul of Fire to each player; if there are Caliburni swords, this imbues one set with fire, and another with ice

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31333
data.value: telegraphed line AoEs leaving swords that persist outside the arena. After a short time, the swords shoot back across

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31349
data.value: Line and roomwide AoE; One side of the arena will be hit with fire, the other with ice. Stand OUTSIDE of the line telegraph, and on the side opposite of the buff you have. This will change your buff.

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31338
data.value: Setup cast - nothing actually happens

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31327
data.value: pointblank AoE with late telegraph - get out; followed by arc or conal AoEs

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31328
data.value: donut AoE with late telegraph - get in; followed by arc or conal AoEs

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_1733
data.value: conal AoEs with Solid/Empty Souls' Caliber

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31330
data.value: indicates Frost's Revelation will happen after next Souls' Caliber, so you'll need to get the fire buff

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31329
data.value: indicates Flame's Revelation will happen after next Souls' Caliber, so you'll need to get the ice buff

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31332
data.value: roomwide ice-aspected AoE - must have fire buff; switches your buff to ice

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31345
data.value: spawns 2 sets of 3 circle AoEs that turn into star AoEs. Outside the center ring of the arena, there is a pattern with 8 points. Each of these points is a safe spot for all possible patterns

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31343
data.value: spawns a series of circles that grow and explode; you can run clockwise around the boss's hitbox to avoid them

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31336
data.value: return attack from Caliburni swords (fire element)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31337
data.value: return attack from Caliburni swords (ice element)

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_91_12100_31347
data.value: line AoEs from Exglacialis stars

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_1_7480_8026
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_1_7480_8025
data.value: inflicts knockback; knockback immunity does not work

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_1_7480_8022
data.value: telegraphed half room AoE; inflicts vulnerability up (40% per stack, max 16 stacks, 45s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_11_7481_390
data.value: instant

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_11_7481_5574
data.value: huge telegraphed conal AoE; inflicts confused (10s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_11_7481_5573
data.value: 360 degree gaze inflicting confused (10s) - look away!

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_11_7481_7850
data.value: telegraphed pointblank AoE; inflicts paralysis (30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_21_7478_11292
data.value: telegraphed line AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_21_7478_6009
data.value: summons Raiuns (untargetable lightning cloud adds)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_21_7478_11291
data.value: proximity AoE; knocks players into the air. Potency scales from 1000 on top of the marker to 100 at ~3y distance (1 boss radius) from the marker edge, so as long as you move a little bit away, damage is minimal

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_21_7478_11293
data.value: 180 degree AoE (covers the entire room in front of the boss)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_21_7478_2368
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m). Vulnerability up is applied even if the damage is fully shielded

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_31_7483_900
data.value: untelegraphed circle AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_31_7483_5582
data.value: telegraphed line AoE; inflicts windburn (magic DoT potency 50, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_31_7483_10966
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE; inflicts windburn (magic DoT potency 50, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_31_7483_10723
data.value: roomwide AoE; inflicts knockback; knockback immunity does not work, but completely blocking the damage with a shield will also block the knockback

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_31_7483_10189
data.value: pointblank AoE; inflicts windburn (magic DoT potency 50, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_51_7487_2329
data.value: telegraphed conal AoE; inflicts knockback

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_51_7487_147
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE; inflicts burns (magic DoT potency 100, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_51_7487_2321
data.value: telegraphed pointblank AoE; inflicts knockback

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_51_7487_1730
data.value: roomwide AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_51_7487_2331
data.value: telegraphed donut AoE; inflicts bleeding (magic DoT potency 50, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_51_7487_2330
data.value: telegraphed line AoE; inflicts bleeding (magic DoT potency 50, 30s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7489_3245
data.value: untelegraphed conal AoE inflicting flesh wound (physical DoT potency 80) - get away or behind

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7489_3241
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7489_3248
data.value: targeted circle AoE; leaves a puddle that inflicts minimum (damage dealt -20%, damage taken +400%). Auto-attacks and Ululation will generally one-shot DPS and healers without steel while in this puddle

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7489_3254
data.value: roomwide AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7489_3247
data.value: telegraphed wide charge AoE to targeted player; inflicts stun (4s)

data.name: AbilityNote_HeavenOnHigh_61_7489_1454

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_21_4562_31841
data.value: spawns adds at the front of the arena that move toward the back, leaving two open spaces

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_21_4562_31843
data.value: wide line AoE down the center of the arena with late telegraph - get OUT. Spawns adds which move from the center to the sides

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_21_4562_31845
data.value: wide line AoEs down both sides of the arena with late telegraph - get IN. Spawns adds which move from each side to the other

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_21_4562_31849
data.value: four sets of telegraphed circle AoEs covering the arena, leaving random spaces open each time; adds spawn from three of the AoEs after each set, moving toward a random player

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_21_4562_31847
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE, with four additional hits chasing the targeted player - keep moving! Spawns an add moving toward a random player on each hit

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_21_4562_7
data.value: triggered whenever you touch an add

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_31_4562_671
data.value: spawns a little tornado under you; instant death if you are standing on it, so make sure you're moving as the cast completes

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_31_4562_31462
data.value: many circle AoEs leaving behind tornados that remain until after Turbine; stepping in one deals damage (magic potency 125) and inflicts windburn (magic DoT potency 250, 6s) which cannot be removed with Esuna

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_31_4562_31465
data.value: four telegraphed circle AoEs on all party members, each a couple seconds apart; make sure to leave a safe path open for Turbine

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_31_4562_4116
data.value: knockback from center

data.name: AbilityNote_EurekaOrthos_31_4562_9906
data.value: boss goes to one end of the arena and charges straight across (line AoE with late telegraph); drops a twister at the same time, so make sure you're moving as the cast completes

data.name: EnemyNote_PilgrimsTraverse_61_14187
data.value: - Spawns in with the Concealed buff, which prevents you from seeing the enemy until you get close (it will see you first if it's looking at you) - Concealed enemies cannot be targeted when invisible, but AoE abilities will hit them as usual, so be careful using AoEs if one of these might be around - Once Concealed is removed, it is never reapplied, so if you deaggro one of these (with sleep, death and Raising, etc.), it will be visible normally afterwards

Inflicts Pyretic (3s); taking any action (including moving or facing a different direction) before the debuff runs out inflicts damage at potency 5000, which is generally a one-shot. The attack can be LoSed

data.name: AbilityWarning_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14207_43322
data.value: other

data.name: EnemyNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14207
data.value: - As of patch 7.35, the Pyretic debuff can trigger even if you remain perfectly still. This seems to be caused by the "Automatically face target when using action" setting in the character target settings, so if you do get hit, make sure to untarget the succubus before it starts moving - It's still safer to LoS or stun - Can only be slowed with Arm's Length if transfigured via Pomander of Witching since it doesn't normally do melee auto-attacks

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14217_40554
data.value: 3 attacks on the target, followed by Heavy Smash

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14217_40555
data.value: 4 attacks on the target, followed by Heavy Smash

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14217_25180
data.value: instant pointblank AoE used after the last Skull Dasher hit

data.name: AbilityWarning_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14217_25180

data.name: EnemyNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14217
data.value: - Same mechanic as the 81+ Humbaba - uses either Triple or Quadruple Skull Dasher followed by Heavy Smash. Count the hits and move away IMMEDIATELY after the last hit to avoid Heavy Smash - The hits from Triple/Quadruple Skull Dasher cannot be LoSed or outranged (the Warg will chase after the target until it gets in range)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14224_1954
data.value: wide line AoE with late telegraph and quick cast

data.name: AbilityWarning_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14224_1954
data.value: directional

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14224_1033
data.value: telegraphed large circle AoE on random player

data.name: EnemyNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14224
data.value: - Uses Gold Dust and Rotten Stench in quick succession - don't sit around after dodging the circle!

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_1_13979_44051
data.value: spawns 3 Striking Shrublet adds

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_1_13979_44053
data.value: large pointblank AoE; knocks players upward and inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_1_13979_44055
data.value: large circle AoE on marked points in telegraphed order; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_1_13979_44059
data.value: jumps to the target and inflicts Down for the Count (1s stun)

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_001
data.value: - Rotation (56s per cycle): - Branch Out - Adds move in random patterns, eventually ending up with two on one side of the arena and one on the other - Hedge Mazing (boss and adds simultaneously) - Adds become targetable and start auto-attacking - Leafmash (4 telegraphs, then 4 hits in the same order) - Any adds still alive become untargetable and use Little Leafmash at the same time each Leafmash hit lands, then die after Leafmash completes - If any adds live long enough to use Little Leafmash, they will probably wipe a solo player either from the damage itself or from being stunlocked and caught in the boss's Leafmash. Most jobs will need to save some cooldowns to burst down the adds in time - Barkbalm incense will help, but don't count on it as a guaranteed kill for the adds because Feo Ul's lightning ability doesn't sync with the boss rotation. The adds are immune to mazeroot (and pomander) witching

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_11_13973_564
data.value: knocks players back about 3/4 the distance from the center to the outer damage ring

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_11_13973_764
data.value: circle AoE around each of boss's feet in telegraphed order; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_11_13973_43460
data.value: circle AoE on marked player

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_11_13973_43463
data.value: 3 circle AoEs on random players (random points near players if the party is smaller than 3 people)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_11_13973_8666
data.value: hits players who step in the roots once per second; inflicts heavy

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_011
data.value: - Rotation (59s per cycle): - Touchdown - The boss's feet will each flash once during the cast, showing the order for Burst explosions - Burst (x4) - Bare Root Planting - Lines of roots slowly extend in 8 directions from this AoE during Dense Planting, disappearing at the end of the rotation - Dense Planting (x5) - The outer damage ring deals ~35k unique damage (unaffected by Steel) every 3 seconds while standing in it, changing to a 15-second DoT for ~13k/tick after leaving - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 5m45s using barkbalm incense, or 9m15s using mazeroot incense, with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_21_13863_39487
data.value: spawns arena damage zones (see rotation)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_21_13863_40401
data.value: half-room AoE with late telegraph on the side of the flashing arm; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_21_13863_40411
data.value: 225-degree AoE with late telegraph covering the opposite side of the arena from Grip of Salvation plus a frontal cone (get behind the glowing arm); knocks players upward and inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_21_13863_44917
data.value: 8 circle AoEs chasing the targeted player - keep moving! Inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_021
data.value: - Initial actions: - Liturgy of Light (5 rings) - The rings descend from above, each causing a Brutal Halo AoE when they land - Grip of Salvation (hits at the same time as the 5th ring) - Salvation's Reach - Liturgy of Light (spinning circles) - Generates a pair of spinning circle zones (either far + middle or far + near) on each cardinal, which then slowly rotate around the arena. Standing in one triggers Bounds of Indulgence once per second - Grip of Salvation - Salvation's Reach - Grip of Salvation - Salvation's Reach - Rotation after opening (57s per cycle): - Divine Favor - Liturgy of Light (6 rings + spinning circles) - Grip of Salvation (hits with the 4th ring) - Salvation's Reach (hits with the 6th ring) - The Liturgy of Light AoEs don't apply vulnerability up, so if you get trapped, it's better to run through one of those than get hit by Grip of Salvation or Salvation's Reach - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 7m using barkbalm incense with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_31_13977_42123
data.value: boss and adds spout yellow or blue particles, telegraphing the order of Shining Shot (yellow) or Saltwater Shot (blue) hits

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_31_13977_42129
data.value: large pointblank AoE with late telegraph; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_31_13977_42128
data.value: knockback

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_31_13977_35486
data.value: pointblank AoE with late telegraph; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_31_13977_35488
data.value: donut AoE with late telegraph; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_31_13977_42130
data.value: telegraphed circle AoEs that spawn randomly across the arena after Near or Far Tide (no cast bar); inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_31_13977_337
data.value: long cast enrage; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m)

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_031
data.value: - Rotation (44s per cycle): - Summons adds (untargetable) - Blown Blessing - The boss will spout either yellow or blue particles, followed by the two adds spouting the same color in random order. After spouting and moving away, the enemies leave markers behind showing where the attacks will hit - The second cycle is always the opposite color of the first. All other cycles are random - Shining Shot OR Saltwater Shot, based on Blown Blessing color - For Shining Shot (yellow), position near the third add and move toward the boss marker after its AoE lands - For Saltwater Shot (blue), position near the boss marker and get knocked toward each add marker in sequence - Adds become targetable after the boss's attack resolves - Far Tide OR Near Tide - Chaser - Not used in the first cycle - These adds are much more lenient than on floor 10, with lower auto-attack damage and more time before their enrage. Most jobs should have no trouble with them - Relying on barkbalm incense can be risky - if you move away for Near Tide, Feo's AoE lightning might miss the adds. It's probably better to stay in the middle and take the hit from Near Tide in this case; the damage isn't dangerous with the barkbalm HP buff, and Feo will heal you up afterwards - A single Arm's Length or Surecast will cover all three blue knockbacks if you use it immediately before the first one - The outer damage ring deals ~45k unique damage (unaffected by Steel) every 3 seconds while standing in it, changing to a 15-second DoT for ~18k/tick after leaving - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 3m50s using barkbalm incense with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14263_19094
data.value: setup cast; turns the floor into quicksand

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14263_43537
data.value: places a countdown marker in the center of the arena which will cast Biting Wind when the countdown completes

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14263_8677
data.value: boss burrows underground, becoming untargetable until the Pit Ambush cast completes

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14263_8675
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE on marked player's position, followed by 3 more untelegraphed circle AoEs chasing the player - keep moving, and don't stay in the quicksand too long!

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14263_7317
data.value: roomwide AoE; inflicts knockback, pushing players into the damage ring. Deals no damage or knockback to players standing in quicksand. Knockback cannot be blocked with Arm's Length / Surecast

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_041
data.value: - Initial actions: - Liquefaction - Falling Rock - Sandpit - Pit Ambush - Falling Rock - Windraiser - Biting Wind - Rotation after opening (42s per cycle): - Windraiser + Falling Rock - Sandpit - Pit Ambush - Biting Wind + Falling Rock - Standing in the quicksand inflicts Six Fulms Under, which kills you if you don't get back on a rock within 4 seconds. It also blocks the effects of Biting Wind, so step into it just long enough to dodge the roomwide cast - Despite the flashy visual effects, Liquefaction and Windraiser deal no damage even if you're not standing on a rock - The outer damage ring deals ~50k unique damage (unaffected by Steel) every 3 seconds while standing in it, changing to a 15-second DoT for ~21k/tick after leaving - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 5m15s using barkbalm incense with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_51_14097_44866
data.value: untelegraphed frontward and backward conal; inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_51_14097_44051
data.value: spawns sabotender adds (see rotation)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_51_14097_44862
data.value: causes sabotender adds to rotate one square; inflicts bind on all players until the adds explode

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_51_14097_44863
data.value: causes sabotender adds to rotate two squares; inflicts bind on all players until the adds explode

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_51_14097_44867
data.value: uses Spineshot 10 times in quick succession, rotating 60 degrees after each one; inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_51_14097_21189
data.value: hits the single square the add is in; inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_51_14097_44859
data.value: hits a 3x3 square area around the add; inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_051
data.value: - Initial actions: - Spineshot - Rotation after opening (1m24s per cycle): - Branch Out (4 columns) - Spawns 4 columns of sabotender adds, which will explode a few seconds later in the order spawned. Small sabotenders explode only on their own square, while large ones explode in a 3x3 square area - Adds explode (Several Thousand Needles and Long-range Needles) - Spineshot - Branch Out (checkerboard) - Spawns sabotender adds in a checkerboard pattern. There will always be three large adds, one in the center and two on opposite sides of the outer ring - One-stone March OR Two-stone March - The cast name indicates whether the adds will move one or two squares in the direction indicated by the arrows. Find the square that will be safe and get there before the cast ends - Adds explode - Spinning Needles - These casts go off VERY quickly - if you're far away, you won't be able to keep up even with Sprint. Get in close as soon as the adds are gone and keep moving as the boss rotates - The conals themselves are 60 degrees wide, so the far edge of one cone in the direction of rotation will be the near edge of the next one - To find the safe spot for One/Two-stone March: - Look for the large add in the middle - Find the closer large add in the outer ring (will be diagonally adjacent to the middle large add) - Check the March direction indicated by the arrows - Move backward from the outer ring add the OPPOSITE of the number of squares indicated by the cast name - one square for Two-stone, two squares for One-stone - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 6m30s using barkbalm incense with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_61_13971_39642
data.value: white orbs left behind when the boss casts Glower or Ardorous Eye that explode into a roomwide donut

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_61_13971_43406
data.value: untelegraphed frontal line AoE, starting either close to or far from the boss and moving to the opposite side

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_61_13971_3972
data.value: knockback from the center of the arena

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_61_13971_31872
data.value: eight large conal telegraphs followed by the actual attacks

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_061
data.value: - All abilities other than Disorienting Groan inflict vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m) - Rotation (1m50s per cycle): - Zealous Glower x2 - Ardorous Eye - 2000-mina Swing OR Disorienting Groan - Octuple Swipe - For Glower and Ardorous Eye, pay attention to the movement of the boss's laser; orbs will spawn in that same pattern, then explode a short time later in spawn order. Move to the first orb as soon as it appears, then from orb to orb as they explode - Be careful to also dodge the second Glower, which will cover part of the 4th donut hole from the first Glower - Octuple Swipe always hits all 4 quadrants once, then all 4 quadrants once more, and will never hit the same quadrant twice in a row. If you start outside the first telegraph, then move into each quadrant as it is hit, you can stop at the 5th hit and be safe for the rest of the mechanic - The outer damage ring deals ~80k unique damage (unaffected by Steel) every 3 seconds while standing in it, changing to a 15-second DoT for ~33k/tick after leaving - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 7m30s using barkbalm incense with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_71_13968_43473
data.value: frontward or backward half-room AoE, telegraphed by the direction of the boss's whiskers; inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m). Used during Roaring Ring/Perilous Lair

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_71_13968_43465
data.value: donut AoE with late telegraph - get in! Inflicts paralysis (15s)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_71_13968_43469
data.value: pointblank AoE with late telegraph - get out! Inflicts bleeding (magic DoT potency 50, 15s)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_71_13968_43476
data.value: line AoE between orbs with late telegraph; inflicts paralysis (15s)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_71_13968_43478
data.value: untelegraphed huge pointblank AoE; inflicts paralysis (15s)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_71_13968_43475
data.value: applies a 17s debuff to all players, either Nowhere to Run or Shadows of Death (randomly chosen per player)

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_071
data.value: - Initial actions: - Roaring Ring OR Perilous Lair (with Profane Waul) - Profane Waul does not have a cast bar of its own, but is always used alongside Roaring Ring / Perilous Lair - Rotation after opening (1m14s per cycle): - Stalking Static - Roaring Ring OR Perilous Lair (with Profane Waul) - Prowling Death - Roaring Ring OR Perilous Lair (with Profane Waul) - Resolve debuffs during this cast - Shadows of Death is a purple-colored debuff, also signaled by popup text reading "All who deny Light are condemned to death..." in the center of the screen, causing instant death on expiration. To cleanse the debuff, intentionally take a hit from Profane Waul (the light-colored half-room AoE) while dodging Perilous Lair or Roaring Ring (pointblank/donut) as usual - Profane Waul's damage is reduced to a trivial amount and does not inflict vulnerability up if it hits this debuff - Nowhere to Run is a red-colored stacking debuff, also signaled by popup text reading "Death approaches all who tread forth..." in the center of the screen. The debuff starts at 1 stack and gains a stack every time the player moves a certain distance, triggering instant death if it reaches 8 stacks before expiring. Stay close to the boss and find the nearest safe spot when the pointblank/donut cast starts - Unlike Shadows of Death, this debuff is NOT removed when the boss AoEs resolve, so stay still until you see the debuff expire! - Try to keep the boss near the center of the arena so there's enough room to escape Perilous Lair regardless of whiskers direction - All paralysis and bleeding debuffs are curable, so a pomander of purification can help if you take a hit from a mechanic - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 9m30s using barkbalm incense with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14090_44250
data.value: 270-degree roomwide AoE with late telegraph - safe spot is behind the boss on the side of the glowing hand. Also spawns 4 Arcane Cylinders covering 2 horizontal and 2 vertical rows of the arena, leaving 1 tile of each 4-tile block uncovered

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14090_43796
data.value: line AoE with late telegraph from an Arcane Cylinder

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14090_44258
data.value: AoE with late telegraph covering the 4-tile blocks to the boss's front and back

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14090_44259
data.value: AoE with late telegraph covering the 4-tile blocks to the boss's left and right

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14090_44267
data.value: spawns 4 waves of Meteor AoEs randomly across the arena

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14090_205
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_81_14090_44266
data.value: inflicts knockback across the arena - get close to the boss. Also spawns Arcane Cylinders on 3 of the 4 tile rows

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_081
data.value: - All abilities except Skinflayer inflict vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m) - Rotation (1m4s per cycle): - Backhand - Fore-hind Folly OR Twin-winged Treachery - Arcane Cylinders fire at the same time - Backhand - Twin-winged Treachery OR Fore-hind Folly - Arcane Cylinders fire at the same time - Hot Iron - Skinflayer - Arcane Cylinders fire shortly after the knockback - The boss uses Fore-hind Folly and Twin-winged Treachery once each per rotation (in random order) - The arena is divided into 16 tiles, which can be thought of as 4 diamond-shaped blocks of 2x2 tiles each. Fore-hind Folly and Twin-winged Treachery each cover 2 of these blocks, and the Arcane Cylinders cover 3 of the 4 tiles in each remaining block, leaving 1 safe tile in each of those blocks - The boss becomes untargetable for an instant while jumping to one side of the arena after Hot Iron - don't forget to retarget! - The outer damage zone deals ~90k unique damage (unaffected by Steel) every 3 seconds while standing in it, changing to a 15-second DoT for ~35k/tick after leaving - Feo Ul will defeat the boss in about 9m30s using barkbalm incense with no player assistance

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_4562_685
data.value: 4 circle AoEs, targeting player positions when the cast FINISHES (like Twister) - wait for the cast to complete, then move away. Inflicts suppuration (max HP -10% and damage taken +20%, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_4562_44063
data.value: inflicts Chains of Condemnation (3s) on all players; moving while the debuff is active inflicts an additional 375 potency magic damage every half-second

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_4562_44085
data.value: line AoE with late telegraph on randomly targeted player; inflicts knockback. If the AoE hits a window behind the arena, a Vodoriga Minion add will spawn (up to 2 adds per window)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_4562_44074
data.value: inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_4562_44065
data.value: either a wide line AoE down the center of the arena, or two wide AoEs down the sides of the arena, depending on whether the boss is holding one or two swords; inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_4562_44081
data.value: pulls players to the center of the arena, inflicts bind (3s), then summons ice pillars to enclose the center - get out as soon as bind expires. Players caught in the middle are hit with a follow-up AoE which also inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_4562_44088
data.value: drains HP (healing the boss) from all players. Can be blocked by having the opposite color debuff of the boss using the ability - light debuff for Eminent Grief, dark debuff for Devoured Eater

data.name: FloorsetNote_PilgrimsTraverse_091
data.value: - Initial actions: - Fire/Blade combo - Eminent Grief will start casting either Ball of Fire or Chains of Condemnation, and Devoured Eater will start casting Blade of First Light - These two casts start at the same time, but one will finish more quickly. Check the cast speeds as well as the ability names and boss tells, and dodge appropriately - Rotation after opening (3m30s per cycle): - Bounds of Sin - Spinelash - Abyssal Blaze - The direction in which the boss pulls open the hole (horizontal or vertical) indicates the direction the exaflare explosions will move later - Drain Aether - Both bosses will cast Drain Aether, but one cast will be quicker than the other. Change your light/dark debuff to match them in the proper order - Abyssal Blaze resolves - Bounds of Sin - Fire/Blade combo - Abyssal Blaze - Spinelash - Bounds of Sin - Fire/Blade combo - Drain Aether - Abyssal Blaze resolves - There are several light and dark puddles on the arena floor. Stepping in one of these applies a 3-minute debuff of that color, which inflicts a tiny DoT but also allows you to damage the boss of the opposite color: Light Vengeance lets you hit Eminent Grief, and Dark Vengeance lets you hit Devoured Eater - If you let the debuff expire, you're penalized with a short loss-of-control debuff, but since you have to switch colors for Drain Aether anyway, this should never be a problem. This mechanic is intended more for the Quantum version of the fight, where the debuff timer is shorter - If the bosses' health levels get more than 25% apart, the one with lower health gains a regeneration buff (0.1%/3s per stack) and the other gains a damage-up buff (+30% per stack), stacking once for each percent past 25%, so don't focus all your damage on a single boss - Feo Ul can help here - she'll always attack whichever boss you hit first, letting you then focus on the other one. Most jobs will still outdamage her and need to switch target occasionally, but she makes it less of a chore - Aim Spinelash down the gold lines on the floor to avoid hitting windows. If an add does spawn, make sure to kill it quickly or it can hit you with Terror Eye while you're trapped by Bounds of Sin - To find the safe corner for Abyssal Blaze, check the boss hands direction and crystal layout for the first set of crystals: - The crystals will be in a diagonal line - neither of the corners on that line is safe - If the direction is horizontal, the remaining corner on the north is safe - If the direction is vertical, the remaining corner on the south is safe - Don't fall off the edge!

data.value: - If a Venery targets you for Sanctified Flare, it will chase you until it gets into line-of-sight for the cast, even into hallways and other rooms. Flare can target even through walls between unconnected adjacent rooms, turning the enemy into a pseudo-patrol.

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14153_417
data.value: small pointblank AoE with long cast time and very late telegraph; will one-shot DPS and healers without Steel

data.name: AbilityWarning_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14153_417
data.value: pointblank

data.name: EnemyNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14153
data.value: - Moves very slowly, but its attacks hurt. Keep your distance when possible - Be careful fighting other enemies alongside one of these - it's easy to forget about the Tortoise Stomp cast until the telegraph flashes under you and you die - Easy to deaggro by just running away

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_41_14263_6624
data.value: 6 telegraphed circle AoEs on random points; inflicts vulnerability up (20% per stack, 1m). Has no cast bar, and is mostly used alongside Windraiser and Biting Wind

data.name: AbilityNote_PalaceOfTheDead_11_4987_5829
data.value: instant

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14223_8666
data.value: telegraphed circle AoE on random player

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14223_8665
data.value: conal AoE with late telegraph on random player

data.name: AbilityWarning_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14223_8665
data.value: directional

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14037_685
data.value: 4 circle AoEs, targeting player positions when the cast FINISHES (like Twister) - wait for the cast to complete, then move away. Inflicts suppuration (max HP -10% and damage taken +20%, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14037_44063
data.value: inflicts Chains of Condemnation (3s) on all players; moving while the debuff is active inflicts an additional 375 potency magic damage every half-second

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14037_44085
data.value: line AoE with late telegraph on randomly targeted player; inflicts knockback. If the AoE hits a window behind the arena, a Vodoriga Minion add will spawn (up to 2 adds per window)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14037_44074
data.value: inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14037_44065
data.value: either a wide line AoE down the center of the arena, or two wide AoEs down the sides of the arena, depending on whether the boss is holding one or two swords; inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14037_44081
data.value: pulls players to the center of the arena, inflicts bind (3s), then summons ice pillars to enclose the center - get out as soon as bind expires. Players caught in the middle are hit with a follow-up AoE which also inflicts vulnerability up (30% per stack, 1m)

data.name: AbilityNote_PilgrimsTraverse_91_14037_44088
data.value: drains HP (healing the boss) from all players. Can be blocked by having the opposite color debuff of the boss using the ability - light debuff for Eminent Grief, dark debuff for Devoured Eater
